author: @AndreaDingbatt
text:
Thank you so much, you are an inspiration to us all, and you'd be an amazing teacher!!
(* Hint - hint, my Hubby is a disaster area, even changing a Light bulb!! +I would pay good money to watch this on YouTube!!)
Best Wishes, Andrea, Jasper and George the Pigeon. XxX.

author: @connyb6
text: Thanks for the idea, now I know what to do with my exercise bike: a flashlight! ;) But I think I will use double-sided adhesive tape for mounting!, instead of grinding my flywheel!

author: @TechKnowCSS
text: Get an radio control  brushless speed controller (because it is sensorless no hall sensors) and control it with a rc servo tester, both total about $10 together. and that would make a great motor once again.

author: @weslingm
text: Thank you for focusing in on free energy technology

author: @benniedonald
text: I love how you enjoy doing this. I hacked together a few miscellaneous parts. Made myself a small fan to chase away fumes when I solder. I got that same rush of happiness and adrenaline.

author: @fransmurati2370
text: J

author: @RajeshKakkad
text: ANY dmn coil and magnet can become generator. The point is making an efficient generator. What is next ? Generator from Ferrari, Ford, Toyota, Cadillac and 1000 other cars' fans ?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I am sorry to say this but have totally missed the point of the video. I understand efficiency is the only point for you but others have other requirements and I have a teaching point here that has nothing to do with efficiency but more to do with the speed constant of motors you might be thinking of using

replies.author: @RajeshKakkad
replies.text:
@ThinkingandTinkering 
A fan is almost always a generator too, isn't it ? Taking it out of a BMW and not from a car junk seems to be the value here.

author: @jackchapman6712
text: Cool! Its seems like the cog arrangement would have the least amount of friction between the generator and the flywheel.

replies.text: I am sorry to say this but you must remember to be polite and respectful to others - if you leave another rude comment I will ban you from posting - I don't mind the comment you left for me but I do mind your response to Teknopottu - please take this warning in the spirit it is intended and that is to help create an environment where everyone can post whatever it is they feel like saying as long as it is respectful of others

author: @tinkmarshino
text: Hey Rob, I was remembering (as I watched your video) you are a battery guy.. is the a way to make oxygen/hydrogen fuel cell on the cheap? I tried to look through your videos but you have so many.. Maybe you could do one on the fuel cell? That would be of great value to us DIY guys.. well me any way.. Just a thought since it is right down your alley..
